Finally, suppose |n/5k = 4 and |n/5k−1 = 21, 22, or 23. Then as before, the first condition implies that the last term of the sum in (*) has valuation −(k − 2), while the second condition implies that the second–to– last term in the same sum has valuation −(k– 1). Hence all terms in the sum (*) have 5–valuation strictly higher than —(k – 1), except for the second–to–last term, and therefore Hn has 5–valuation —(k – 1) in this case. In particular, Hn is integral (mod 5) in this case if and only if k ≤ 1, which gives the additional values n = 21, 22, and 23.
